description:
You are a Healthcare Database Administrator responsible for the scheduling systems at a large hospital network. The HOSPITAL_ADMIN schema in Oracle (connected as hosp_admin/HospAdmin2024 to XEPDB1) contains a SURGERY_SCHEDULE table where historical surgeries were double-booked due to a missing constraint. Identify all pairs of non-cancelled surgeries that overlap in time and share either the same room_id or surgeon_id. Save these results in a new table named DOUBLE_BOOKINGS_LOG with columns conflict1_id (the lower schedule_id), conflict2_id (the higher schedule_id), shared_resource_type ('ROOM', 'SURGEON', or 'BOTH'), and the rounded overlap_minutes.

Create a PL/SQL procedure PROC_SCHEDULE_SURGERY (parameters: p_room_id, p_surgeon_id, p_patient_id, p_procedure, p_start_time, p_end_time) to handle new bookings. The procedure must check for overlaps with existing non-cancelled surgeries; if a conflict exists, raise a custom exception using RAISE_APPLICATION_ERROR(-20001, 'Schedule conflict detected'). Otherwise, insert the record into SURGERY_SCHEDULE with status 'SCHEDULED' using SEQ_SURGERY_SCHEDULE.NEXTVAL.

Develop a view ROOM_UTILIZATION_VW that calculates utilized minutes per room per day (columns: room_id, operation_date, utilized_minutes). Use analytical SQL to merge overlapping intervals into continuous blocks to ensure true utilization is calculated without over-counting double-booked time. Export the contents of this view for the entire dataset to /home/ga/Documents/exports/or_utilization.csv.

Connection details: Host=localhost, Port=1521, Service=XEPDB1, User=hosp_admin, Password=HospAdmin2024.
